content.problemStatement: Create a program to calculate and display the total length of two specified words: **Naruto** and **Sasuke**.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T (1 ≤ T ≤ 10), the number of test cases.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single line containing the integer 12.

solution.approach: The words "Naruto" and "Sasuke" each have length 6, so their total length is always 12. The problem therefore reduces to reading the number of test cases T and printing the constant value 12 exactly T times, each on its own line. No additional computation or storage is required.

code.Python3:
import sys
data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
if not data:
    sys.exit()
T = int(data[0])
output = '\\n'.join(['12'] * T)
sys.stdout.write(output)
